exclusions.reason: Dot indicators at sm size (32px height) are too small for legibility; minimum is md
Double exclusion: both constraints apply; horizontal dot at sm is illegible

_decisions.what: Steps is a navigation indicator only, not a wizard container
_decisions.why: 10/14 systems use indicator-only architecture; wizard containers couple step content with metadata making conditional/branching steps hard. Consumers wire step content and focus management independently.
horizontal vs. vertical produce structurally different layouts (row vs. column, horizontal vs. vertical connector line) — structural change → variant required
number / icon / dot show different indicator shapes and content — structural change requiring distinct Figma frames
Interactive toggles whether step items use <button>/<a> vs. <span> — no structural layout change; SpaceEqually changes flex distribution — both are binary config toggles, not structural changes
Steps is a container; its content is composed of Step sub-component instances. The slot holds repeated Step instances.

_decisions.what: radius=9999 (circle) for indicator circles at all sizes
_decisions.why: Step indicators are circular (number circle, dot); pill/full-radius is the standard shape across all 14 systems with the component

_decisions.what: frame net=16 within medium budget (30-80) for container
_decisions.why: 2 orientations × 3 sizes × 3 indicatorTypes = 18 gross minus 2 excluded dot×sm combos = 16 net; Steps container itself is simple — complexity lives in the Step sub-component

exclusions.reason: Error and warning are mutually exclusive on the same step; a step cannot simultaneously be blocking and non-blocking
Disabled implies locked (cannot be reached); wait simply means not-yet-reached — these are semantically overlapping; use Disabled for locked steps, wait for unreached steps
Finished steps replace the number with a checkmark — IndicatorType=check is the correct indicator for finish status
Error steps replace the number with an error icon — IndicatorType=error-icon is the correct indicator for error status
Error and check/finish indicators are mutually exclusive
Finish and error indicators are mutually exclusive

_decisions.what: Step is a sub-component with isSubComponent=true
_decisions.why: 4+ systems (Carbon, Atlassian, Ant Design, Mantine) use named Step child items. Step has its own states (Status), its own indicator slot (indicator), and its own optional description — sub-component detection rule applies

_decisions.what: Status as variant dimension (not boolean or state)
_decisions.why: Status is SYSTEM feedback — each value (wait/process/finish/error/warning) communicates a system/data condition. Status changes fill color, icon, connector color, and text weight — ≥2 visual properties → variant per global-property-rules

_decisions.what: IndicatorType as variant dimension on Step (not just Steps container)
_decisions.why: Step's IndicatorType controls the content of the indicator slot — number shows a digit, check shows a checkmark icon, error-icon shows ⚠, custom-icon swaps with instance, dot shows a circle. Structural content change → variant
Disabled on a step (locked non-navigable step) changes opacity + cursor + removes interactive affordance. ≤1 structural property change → boolean per global-property-rules disabled decision tree
Description is optional text below the label. Its presence is a binary on/off toggle without structural layout change beyond height — boolean extraction per Technique 1

_decisions.what: indicator slot uses BOOL+SWAP for custom-icon IndicatorType
_decisions.why: When IndicatorType=custom-icon, the indicator content is an instance swap accepting any icon; boolean Show Custom Icon controls visibility, swap Indicator Icon controls which icon
Description needs both a visibility boolean (HasDescription) and an editable text layer — boolText is the correct pairing

_decisions.what: Status=finish mapped to IndicatorType=check (exclusion of number)
_decisions.why: Universal DS consensus: completed steps replace the step number with a checkmark icon. Number stays only for wait/process statuses.
5 statuses × 5 indicatorTypes = 25 gross minus 6 logically impossible status×indicator combos = 19 net; Step sub-component is a medium-complexity atomic element
Within medium component budget (30-80); Steps container focuses on layout/orientation while Step carries status/indicator complexity
